verses.text: Who remembers this house in her first glory? In their eyes the new is 'as nothing.' Discouragement. The work of God can look small beside the memory of past splendour.
Who remembers this house in her first glory? In their eyes the new is 'as nothing.' Discouragement. The work of God can look small beside the memory of past splendour.
Who remembers this house in her first glory? In their eyes the new is 'as nothing.' Discouragement. The work of God can look small beside the memory of past splendour.
Yet be strong, Zerubbabel, Joshua, and all the people, 'for I am with you'; My Spirit remains, fear not. Strength in His presence. God's nearness is the cure for a discouraged heart.
Yet be strong, Zerubbabel, Joshua, and all the people, 'for I am with you'; My Spirit remains, fear not. Strength in His presence. God's nearness is the cure for a discouraged heart.
Once more He will shake heaven and earth, and 'the desire of all nations shall come,' and He will fill this house with glory. The Messiah. Christ Himself would honour this temple with His presence.
Once more He will shake heaven and earth, and 'the desire of all nations shall come,' and He will fill this house with glory. The Messiah. Christ Himself would honour this temple with His presence.

The silver and gold are His; 'the glory of this latter house shall be greater than of the former,' and there He will give peace. Surpassing glory. The plainer house was greater, for Christ would stand in it.

The silver and gold are His; 'the glory of this latter house shall be greater than of the former,' and there He will give peace. Surpassing glory. The plainer house was greater, for Christ would stand in it.
He asks the priests: does holy flesh in a skirt make other things holy? They answer, No. Holiness limited. Mere contact with the sacred does not sanctify.
He asks the priests: does holy flesh in a skirt make other things holy? They answer, No. Holiness limited. Mere contact with the sacred does not sanctify.
He asks the priests: does holy flesh in a skirt make other things holy? They answer, No. Holiness limited. Mere contact with the sacred does not sanctify.
But does one defiled by a corpse make things unclean? Yes; 'so is this people,' and their offerings, before Me. Defilement spreads. Uncleanness, unlike holiness, passes readily to others.
But does one defiled by a corpse make things unclean? Yes; 'so is this people,' and their offerings, before Me. Defilement spreads. Uncleanness, unlike holiness, passes readily to others.
Consider: before the foundation was laid, He smote their crops with blasting and hail, 'yet ye turned not to me.' Chastening ignored. God's judgments were meant to turn them back.
Consider: before the foundation was laid, He smote their crops with blasting and hail, 'yet ye turned not to me.' Chastening ignored. God's judgments were meant to turn them back.
Consider: before the foundation was laid, He smote their crops with blasting and hail, 'yet ye turned not to me.' Chastening ignored. God's judgments were meant to turn them back.
Consider from the day the foundation was laid: the barn is empty still, but 'from this day will I bless you.' Blessing begins. Obedience opens the door to God's favour.
Consider from the day the foundation was laid: the barn is empty still, but 'from this day will I bless you.' Blessing begins. Obedience opens the door to God's favour.
A second word to Zerubbabel: God will shake kingdoms and 'make thee as a signet: for I have chosen thee.' The chosen line. Through Zerubbabel the promise runs on to Christ, God's signet.
